title: The Typing Spectrum: Static vs. Dynamic
overview: Do we check the types before the program runs (Static) or while it’s running (Dynamic)? Static systems offer safety; dynamic systems offer speed of iteration.
historical_context: The debate has raged since the early days of FORTRAN (Static) and Lisp (Dynamic).
mental_model: Static: A rigid blueprint checked before construction. Dynamic: Building as you go, checking compatibility at each step.
key_innovations: JIT compilation for performance in dynamic systems.
Gradual typing for the middle ground.
tradeoffs: Static: Upfront effort, ceremony. Dynamic: Runtime errors, potential for hidden bugs.
legacy: Most modern languages now seek a hybrid approach (e.g., TypeScript, Python Type Hints).
ai_assisted_discovery_missions: Explain the impact of Strong vs. Weak typing on the Static/Dynamic axis.
How did the rise of TypeScript change the industry’s perception of dynamic typing?
Analyze how JIT compilers (V8, PyPy) optimize dynamic types at runtime.
